component.name: k-rgb
component.summary: Control the RGB lighting on the Alienware AW410K keyboard

description.p: k-rgb is a native KDE application for controlling the per-key RGB lighting of the Alienware AW410K mechanical keyboard on Linux. It talks directly to the keyboard over hidraw — no vendor software and no root daemon.

ul.li: Solid colour and a graphical per-key editor you paint directly
Named profiles you can switch from the window or the system tray
Per-key static rainbow across all 107 keys
Hardware effects: breathing, pulse, spectrum, single and rainbow waves, scanner
Brightness control and a system-tray quick-switcher
Remembers your lighting and restores it at login

description.p: Adds a graphical per-key colour editor and named profiles you can switch from the window or the system tray.
First release: solid colour, per-key rainbow, hardware effects, brightness, system tray, and restore-on-login.
